figures.caption: Figure 2: Examples of 2D slices. a): Real, b): Corresponding sparse manual annotation, c)-e): Segmentation masks predicted by the pretrained segmentation models, and f)-i): Segmentation masks by our models.
Figure 3: Examples of zoomed-in 2D slices. a): Real, b)-h): Segmentation masks predicted for this instance by the segmentation models included in this study.

Figure 1: 3D renderings illustrating the synthesis pipeline. Real myotube data ranges from sparsely distributed (a) to heavily overlapping configurations (b). The pipeline generates instance-labeled annotation volumes (c) and corresponding synthetic fluorescence image stacks (d) that closely approximate real imaging conditions.
